subject: Make aio.sh suitable for CI
commit_message:
Make aio.sh suitable for CI

aio.sh script currently clones multicloud/k8s repo itself.
That is unnessary and makes it impossible to hook k8s repository
to CI system where commit changeset is used to define repository
files to be tested.

This commit will remove cloning of multicloud/k8s repository
and also move aio.sh to baremetal folder which seems to be
the intended place for it.

Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-629

Change-Id: Ifc54bbc1ec06bc4c3fbbecca7e676d5ffa392d78
Signed-off-by: Samuli Silvius <s.silvius@partner.samsung.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

This patch is depending on the fact that baremetal folder and vagrant folder are at same relative location. This assumption should be broken

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> This patch is depending on the fact that baremetal folder and vagrant folder are at same relative location. This assumption should be broken

Ok any suggestion how to do it then? Normally it's good to fix locations relative in git same way like many scripts does by running another scripts located in same folder (same relative dependency).

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
 > >
 > > This patch is depending on the fact that baremetal folder and
 > vagrant folder are at same relative location. This assumption
 > should be broken
 > 
 > Ok any suggestion how to do it then? Normally it's good to fix
 > locations relative in git same way like many scripts does by
 > running another scripts located in same folder (same relative
 > dependency).

It would be good to bring out installer script from the vagrant folder as it is not only used in vagrant case but also in baremetal. Is it hard to break installer.sh folder dependencies?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
>  > >
>  > > This patch is depending on the fact that baremetal folder and
>  > vagrant folder are at same relative location. This assumption
>  > should be broken
>  > 
>  > Ok any suggestion how to do it then? Normally it's good to fix
>  > locations relative in git same way like many scripts does by
>  > running another scripts located in same folder (same relative
>  > dependency).
> 
> It would be good to bring out installer script from the vagrant folder as it is not only used in vagrant case but also in baremetal. Is it hard to break installer.sh folder dependencies?

Ok that way. Yes it will bigger re-factoring. But I can do that but it's better to do in following commit and let this one first merged.

Then I could bring installer.sh and other related scripts under kud folder e.g. install folder and hosting_providers folder at same level would contain Vagrant and baremetal folders both utilizing script in install.

Like this:

├── kud
│   ├── demo
│   │   └── firewall
│   ├── deployment_infra
│   │   ├── galaxy-requirements.yml
│   │   └── playbooks
│   ├── hosting_providers
│   │   ├── baremetal    <<-- aio.sh here
│   │   └── vagrant      <<-- Vagrant file and related config etc. here
│   ├── install          <<-- NEW folder on top level. install.sh and other scripts here.
│   ├── README.md
│   ├── sites
│   └── tests
...

What you think?
